steps.assistant_text: __rmonClearBreak block-1 fragment (21 insns / 0x54, TRUNCATE_TEXT=0x100; block 2 = func_800081D0 in kernel_020_c.c). Was a documented NON_MATCHING cap at 3 diffs (formerly INSN_PATCH'd, banned). Re-classified the diffs: NOT pure coloring -- two distinct C-fixable levers. (1) SPILL-PLACEMENT: the target homes the masked BREAK-test value `*saved_a` (t8) into a dead phantom stack slot 28(sp), written once in the bne delay slot and never reloaded; the prior C homed the saved_a POINTER (t7) in the wrong (beq) delay slot. Naming the dereferenced compare value as its own local `instr_a` while reading the pointer FRESH from rmonbrk_bss_0000[0] for the stores (no pointer caching) reproduces the exact phantom-slot home + delay-slot fill -- 3 diffs -> 1 diff. Caching the pointer in a local instead grows the frame +8 and adds a real spill. (2) CROSS-FRAGMENT BRANCH: the remaining 1 diff was the beqz displacement (build +0x17 vs target +0x18) -- a per-.o objdump artifact of truncation, but it is baked into the .o word and so a REAL miss. Block 1's `rmonbrk_bss_0000 = 0` tail was emitting one fewer instruction than the target's lui+addiu+sw pointer-form store; writing it as `((s32*)&rmonbrk_bss_0000)[0] = 0` (array/pointer form) forces the 3-insn materialization, lengthening block 1 by one instruction so the cross-fragment branch lands at +0x18. Linked-ELF objdump: all 21 words byte-identical to asm/nonmatchings/kernel/func_8000817C.s including the `.word 0x11E00018` cross-fragment beqz; objdiff fuzzy 100.0.
